validation_cycles.overview: The Data Architecture Agent performs self-validation before writing plan/data-architecture.md. All validation checks must pass before the artifact is considered complete.

check.name: entity_completeness
check.description: Every data-bearing component from architecture and HLD has corresponding entity definitions.

criteria.criterion: All entities referenced in HLD or architecture have schema definitions.
No "TBD" or placeholder entities in the spec.

check.name: relationship_correctness
check.description: Relationships between entities are correctly defined.

criteria.criterion: Foreign keys and cardinality are explicit.
No circular dependencies without clear resolution.
Relationships align with HLD data flows.

check.name: index_coverage
check.description: All identified query patterns have index support.

criteria.criterion: Every query pattern from HLD maps to an index or access path.
Index definitions include columns and order where relevant.

check.name: lifecycle_defined
check.description: Data lifecycle is documented for all entities or categories.

criteria.criterion: Retention policy defined (or explicitly "indefinite" with rationale).
Archival and deletion strategy documented where applicable.

check.name: migration_strategy_present
check.description: Schema evolution and migration approach is documented.

criteria.criterion: Migration strategy for schema changes is defined.
Rollback approach for failed migrations is documented.

validation_flow.step: Run all self-validation checks after access_patterns phase.
If any check fails, iterate on the design before writing.
Do not write plan/data-architecture.md until all checks pass.
Report validation status to the user upon completion.
